records.summary: A frontal chest X-ray of a 79-year-old male patient, showing the bony thorax, lung fields, and mediastinum. The image includes patient identification data on the left side.
key_observations: Patient is 79 years old (79YM) and male (SEX: M)
Image is labeled 'CHEST' at the bottom left
Standard PA view of chest with visible ribs, clavicles, and lung fields
Image appears to be a digital radiograph displayed on a monitor

records.stage_evidence: Radiology report with findings and impression

ocr.verbatim:
X-RAY CHEST (PA VIEW)
Patient name:-
Age:- 79y
Date:- 27/03/2026
Sex:- male
Findings:-
• Bilateral lung fields shows increase in vascular markings with patchy opacities seen in right upper lobe.
• Bilateral lung appear hyperinflated.
• Trachea slightly shifted towards left
• Cardiac size are within normal limits.
• Mediastinum contours appears maintained and right side diaphragm appears slightly elevated .
Impression:-
• Above features suggestive of bronchitis likely allergic/infective ?copd
Clinical correlation suggested
Signature
TIMING: 8.00 AM TO 8.00 PM FOR HOME COLLECTION-DIAL Mob. : 9795819823, 8604505116
24 घण्टे अल्ट्रासाउण्ड सुविधा उपलब्ध
सुविधाएँ: सारी प्रकार के पेशीलॉजिकल जांचे • हॉर्मोन्स • ग्लूकोसाइलेटेड हीमोग्लोबिन (Hb 1c) • ट्यूबल टेस्ट
Clinical correlation is essential for final diagnosis, in case of disparity test must be repeated. this report is not valid for medicolegal purpose.

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a PA chest X-ray performed on a 79-year-old male patient on March 27, 2026. The findings include increased vascular markings, patchy opacities in the right upper lobe, hyperinflated lungs, leftward tracheal shift, normal cardiac size, and a slightly elevated right diaphragm. The impression suggests bronchitis, possibly allergic or infective, with a question of COPD. The report advises clinical correlation and notes it is not valid for medicolegal purposes.
key_observations: Findings: Hyperinflated lungs, patchy opacities in right upper lobe, leftward tracheal shift, normal cardiac size, elevated right diaphragm
Impression: Suggestive of bronchitis (allergic/infective) or COPD
Report advises clinical correlation and is not valid for medicolegal use
